label: Spotify SongMetadata
summary: A single Spotify SongMetadata addon is the honest first implementation shape because direct track-id lookup and bounded search fallback both belong to one provider-backed adapter, not to multiple fragmented Spotify rows.
The right backend is Spotify's official TypeScript SDK, which lets Axolync wrap a maintained provider client instead of hand-writing another Spotify Web API integration.
This is valuable but more operationally sensitive than iTunes because auth ownership and token lifecycle are part of the implementation cost.

sources.label: GitHub: Spotify Web API TS SDK

signals: Spotify documents `duration_ms` directly on the track object.
The official TypeScript SDK is maintained by Spotify and fits a wrapper-first addon implementation.
Search fallback is useful, but auth policy and result precision are the real reasons this is not as simple as iTunes.

notes: The implementation should wrap Spotify's official OSS SDK, but the decisive dependency is still Spotify's hosted Web API and platform policy.

entries.subject: Spotify Get Track API
notes: The adapter depends on Spotify Web API access and Spotify platform rules.

entries.subject: spotify/spotify-web-api-ts-sdk

evidence.label: GitHub: Spotify Web API TS SDK

notes: This official TypeScript SDK is the intended wrapper backend for the addon.
Official API quality and official SDK support are strong.
Auth ownership and token lifecycle are the main complexity.

methods.notes: Possible with browser-owned auth, but auth complexity is the limiting factor.
There is no engine to port.
A native bridge is not the natural answer unless it already owns Spotify credentials.
Feasible, but awkward compared with a proper host or browser-owned auth lane.
Too brittle for an authenticated provider integration.
Can own credentials, but adds a moving part when a shared host could do the same more cleanly.
Works, but the strongest wrapper evidence is in TypeScript, not Python.
The cleanest way to wrap the official TypeScript SDK while centralizing token ownership and caching.
A CLI shell around Spotify adds indirection without improving the core integration.
A remote metadata broker can also own auth, but is heavier than a shared node host.
Viable, but more operational cost than the first Spotify SongMetadata problem needs.
This candidate is about the Spotify Web API, not a platform SDK.

notes: Wrap the official TypeScript SDK and keep direct id lookup plus bounded search fallback in one adapter.
Leave scrape-style Spotify helper paths out of the first implementation seed.

openQuestions: Where should Spotify auth live in the first implementation?
Which accepted-match Spotify affinity inputs should allow the adapter to run?
How conservative should Spotify search fallback be when no direct Spotify track id is available?
